methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Deep Atomic is a pre-seed nuclear energy company founded by William G.J. Theron. The nuclear energy sector comprises 11 identified comparables: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median approach is not applicable to this valuation. While 8 same-sector nuclear energy peers exist, none satisfy narrow stage-matching filters. Zero companies qualified as same-stage pre-seed comparables, zero as adjacent-stage, and zero as similar-valuation-tier peers. The standard threshold for peer-median applicability requires a minimum of three datapoints within constrained parameters; this dataset cannot meet that criterion. An all-sector median would introduce material bias and is therefore not defensible.

Given the inapplicability of primary triangulation methods, valuation of Deep Atomic cannot be anchored to peer multiples at this time. No credible quantitative range is supported by the available data. Future valuation work should focus on either identifying pre-seed or seed-stage nuclear energy peers with disclosed valuations, or applying alternative methods (comparable transactions, DCF sensitivity analysis, or venture method frameworks) once the company has disclosed operational or technical milestones. The scarcity of pre-seed-stage nuclear energy valuations in public sources represents a material constraint on precision.
